text: Master athletes (>40 years old) have improved their endurance performance at a faster rate than younger counterparts over recent decades.
Age-related declines in endurance performance are smaller in cycling than in running and swimming.
Age-related declines in physiological function do not differ significantly between males and females, as evidenced by relatively stable gender differences across ages.
Maximal oxygen consumption (VO2max) is the physiological determinant of endurance performance most altered by aging.
Exercise economy and lactate threshold decline to a lesser extent with advancing age compared to VO2max.
Maintaining a high exercise-training stimulus with advancing age is the single most important means of limiting the rate of decline in endurance performance.
